summary: Successfully tested the new Job Card fields: 'Service Number' and 'Kilometers Driven'. Both fields are correctly implemented in the frontend form and backend API. All tests passed.
critical_code_review_comments: The new fields are correctly implemented as optional fields in the Service model (service_number: Optional[str], kms_driven: Optional[int])
Frontend form correctly places 'Kilometers Driven' in Vehicle Information section and 'Service Number' in Service Details section
Both fields are properly initialized in newJobCardData state and sent to the API on form submission
seed_data_creation: Created test job cards: JOB-000145 (via API), JOB-000146 (via UI) with service_number and kms_driven fields
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two new fields were added to the Job Card form: 1) 'Service Number' (service_number) in Service Details section, 2) 'Kilometers Driven' (kms_driven) in Vehicle Information section. Both fields are optional and work correctly. Test job cards JOB-000145 and JOB-000146 were created during testing.

test_details.feature_description: Added two new fields to the 'Open New Job Card' form: Service Number and Kilometers Driven

test_steps_performed: 1. Verified backend API health and login
2. Created job card via API with service_number='SRV-TEST-001' and kms_driven=15000 - SUCCESS
3. Navigated to Services -> Job Cards tab
4. Clicked 'Add New Job' button to open modal
5. Verified 'Kilometers Driven' field exists in Vehicle Information section - FOUND
6. Verified 'Service Number' field exists in Service Details section - FOUND
7. Filled form with test data including new fields (service_number='SRV-UI-TEST-001', kms_driven=25000)
9. Verified data persisted correctly via API - CONFIRMED
10. Ran pytest tests for backend API - 6/6 PASSED
